semanticCore: Weakness, despised, thin, serving, humbleness, or low fluid
quranicRelevance: Despised fluid of semen (Maheen - 'ma-in maheenin'), and a worthless/despised oath-taker ('hallafin maheenin').

keyDerivations.meaning: Despised, weak, worthless, low, insignificant, or humble

figurativeOrRareUsages: ثُمَّ جَعَلَ نَسْلَهُ مِنْ سُلَالَةٍ مِنْ مَاءٍ مَهِينٍ (32:8): Then He made his progeny from an extract of a despised/weak fluid (humble origin of human creation).
discardedNoise: Professional careers (Mihnah) - spelling overlap in root.
summaryEn: The root represents lowliness (Mahanah), describing the humble fluid of reproduction (Maheen) to challenge human pride and condemning worthless, lying flatterers.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر القلة والضعف والحقارة والمهانة، واصفاً سلالة التكاثر بالماء المهين الضعيف تنبيهاً للإنسان عن كبره، وذاماً حلاف المذلة (مهين).
rawText: اِمْتَهَنَهُ 8 He used it for service and work: (K:) i. q. اِبْتَذَلَهُ, q. v.: (S, Msb:) He held it in mean estimation. (Har, p. 65.) مَهِينٌ Contemptible; abject: (S, K) weak: having little judgment and discrimination. (K.) مَِهْنَةٌ [is syn. with عَمَلٌ and فِعْلٌ, and means work, labour, or] service; ministration; performance of an office. (S, &c.) === Also, The clothes worn in service, or in the performance of business. (Msb.)
